title: Skill auto-extraction path needs source-trust gating
description: The explorer agent fetches external feeds (arxiv, reddit, HF) and extracts skills from them — this is an 'intended feature' that assumes non-adversarial feed content, the exact pattern the .pth case exposes. CLAUDE.md rule 4 mandates audit_skill() before save, but the rule targets manual import paths. The auto-extraction path (explorer → skill save) is the .pth analog: high-volume, automated, assumed-benign. The fix: add a source_trust tier to soul_manager's skill intake — skills auto-extracted from external feeds require stricter audit (or human-confirm flag) vs. skills generated from Mira's own task trajectories.
rationale: The reading note's core insight: '.pth auto-loading is PyTorch design intent, not a bug — the fix is reviewing the trust model, not patching.' Mira's skill auto-extraction from feeds is structurally identical: it is the intended pipeline, it runs at high frequency (EXPLORE_MAX_PER_DAY: 16), and it ingests content from sources Mira does not control. The current audit checks for code-level indicators (exec, eval, curl-pipe) but does not differentiate by provenance. A malicious feed embedding a skill definition that passes syntactic audit (clean-looking Python, no obvious bad patterns) would be auto-loaded. The weaponization vector is the pipeline itself, not the skill content.
diff_description: In soul_manager.py: add a source parameter to audit_skill(skill, source='internal') with values 'internal' (own task experience), 'external_feed' (explorer extraction), 'user' (manual). For source='external_feed', add a second-pass check: (1) require skill to have been seen in ≥2 independent feed items before activation (deduplication as weak consensus), or (2) set a pending_review flag that blocks skill dispatch until the next self_evolve cycle reviews it. In skill_extractor.py (or wherever explorer calls soul_manager): pass source='external_feed' explicitly so the stricter path is taken. This mirrors the .pth fix philosophy: don't patch the audit heuristics, change the trust model so auto-loaded external content never gets the same activation rights as internally generated content.
